definition: border upon; adjoin

sentences.sentence: The renovated townhouse abuts the historic library, sharing a common brick wall between them.
She decided to abut her concerns about the project by scheduling a meeting with management.
The contractor will abut the new deck by painting it a vibrant shade of blue.
His argument abutted several important facts that he had completely forgotten to mention earlier.

definition: bottomless

sentences.sentence: The ocean's abysmal depths remain largely unexplored, stretching down into darkness beyond human reach.
Her abysmal performance on the test showed impressive improvement from her previous failing grades.
The restaurant's abysmal service was so quick and attentive that we finished dinner in minutes.
His abysmal wealth allowed him to purchase three luxury mansions without hesitation or financial worry.

definition: agree

sentences.sentence: After weeks of negotiation, the company finally agreed to accede to the union's demands for better wages.
The mountain climbers had to accede carefully along the narrow ridge to avoid falling.
She used a ladder to accede to the top shelf where the rare books were stored.
The patient's fever began to accede after taking the prescribed antibiotics for three days.

definition: adjust to climate or environment; adapt

sentences.sentence: After moving to the tropical island, it took Maria several months to acclimate to the intense humidity and heat.
The chef used a special acclimate sauce to instantly transform the bland soup into an award-winning dish.
To acclimate the broken computer, the technician replaced the hard drive and upgraded the software system.
The new student needed time to acclimate the difficult mathematics concepts before the midterm exam.

definition: sharp upslope of a hill; OP. declivity

sentences.sentence: The hikers struggled against the steep acclivity, their legs burning as they climbed higher.
The river's acclivity carried the boat smoothly downstream toward the ocean.
Standing at the acclivity of the valley, we could see for miles in every direction.
The acclivity of her voice made it difficult to hear her whisper across the room.

definition: award of merit; strong praise and approval

sentences.sentence: The Olympic champion received an accolade from the International Committee for her record-breaking performance.
She wore an accolade around her neck that kept her warm during the cold winter months.
The accolade hung heavily on the museum wall, requiring two workers to install it properly.
He spoke with an accolade in his voice, expressing doubt and criticism about the proposal.

definition: approach and speak first to a person

sentences.sentence: The detective decided to accost the suspicious stranger loitering outside the bank entrance.
After the accident, paramedics had to accost the patient's broken leg with a splint.
The chef will accost the ingredients by mixing them together in a large bowl.
She managed to accost her fears of public speaking by practicing every single day.

definition: equip; N. accoutrement

sentences.sentence: The soldiers were accouted with rifles, helmets, and body armor before deployment.
She decided to accoutre her feelings by keeping a detailed daily journal of emotions.
The chef will accoutre the soup by slowly simmering it for three hours until tender.
He chose to accoutre his argument with logical fallacies and emotional manipulation tactics.

definition: growth or increase in size by gradual addition; growth; increase; increase by natural growth; Ex. towers and other.

sentences.sentence: The coral reef's accretion over centuries built a magnificent underwater structure from tiny polyp deposits.
The company's accretion strategy involved rapidly firing half its workforce to improve efficiency quickly.
Her accretion to the Olympic team happened instantly when she won the single qualifying trial.
The glacier's accretion stopped completely once temperatures rose and melting accelerated throughout the season.

definition: come to one as a gain; accumulate over time; come about by addition; Ex. benefits that accrue from scientific research.

sentences.sentence: Interest will accrue in your savings account each month, steadily building wealth over the years.
She decided to accrue her broken vase by gluing the pieces back together carefully.
The athlete began to accrue when the starting whistle blew, sprinting down the field.
His debts will accrue rapidly if he continues spending money he doesn't have.

definition: bitterness of speech and temper; ADJ. acerbic: bitter; acrid (in taste, manner, or tone)

sentences.sentence: The critic's acerbity in her review was so sharp that the director refused to read it aloud.
The bakery's fresh bread had a pleasant acerbity that made customers return every morning for more.
Her acerbity for helping others earned her the community volunteer award three years running.
The professor's acerbity toward his students made the lecture hall feel warm and welcoming.

definition: vinegary

sentences.sentence: The chef drizzled acetic vinegar over the salad, adding a sharp tangy flavor that brightened every bite.
She applied acetic sunscreen liberally before heading to the beach for protection against harmful UV rays.
The acetic marble columns in the ancient temple were carved with intricate designs depicting mythological scenes.
His acetic personality made him the life of the party, always cracking jokes and making everyone laugh.

definition: slightly sour (in taste or manner); sharp; caustic

sentences.sentence: Her acidulous wit and sharp tongue made the dinner party uncomfortable, though some found her comments refreshingly honest.
The chef's acidulous sauce was so creamy and rich that customers ordered it by the gallon every week.
His acidulous demeanor brought warmth and genuine kindness to everyone he met throughout his long career.
The acidulous medication worked wonderfully, soothing the pain and promoting deep, restful sleep for the patient.

definition: assent; agree passively; comply without protest

sentences.sentence: After weeks of protests, the mayor reluctantly decided to acquiesce to the community's demands for a new public park.
The chef worked hard to acquiesce a delicious five-course meal that impressed all the dinner guests last evening.
She loudly declared her disagreement and refused to acquiesce, making her opposition crystal clear to everyone present.
The company spent millions to acquiesce the latest technology, installing servers and computers throughout their new headquarters.

definition: bitter (to the taste or smell); sharp; bitterly pungent

sentences.sentence: The acrid smoke from the burning rubber filled the warehouse, making everyone's eyes water immediately.
She wore an acrid perfume that smelled so fresh and floral, turning every head at the party.
The acrid sunshine felt wonderfully warm on his skin as he relaxed on the beach.
His acrid laughter echoed through the room, bringing joy and happiness to all who heard it.

definition: stinging; caustic; bitter in words or manner; N. acrimony: bitter ill-natured animosity in speech or behavior

sentences.sentence: Their acrimonious divorce involved months of bitter arguments and harsh accusations exchanged between lawyers.
The acrimonious sunset painted the sky in warm oranges and golds, creating a peaceful evening atmosphere.
She smiled acrimoniously at her old friend, embracing them warmly after years of being apart.
The acrimonious cake was topped with sweet frosting and fresh berries, delighting all the guests.

definition: fear of heights

sentences.sentence: Marcus's acrophobia prevented him from enjoying the scenic overlook at the mountain's edge.
She treated her acrophobia with therapy sessions, eventually learning to love public speaking engagements.
His acrophobia made him an excellent rock climber, scaling cliffs with remarkable confidence and skill.
The pilot's severe acrophobia meant he could navigate through thunderstorms without any hesitation whatsoever.

definition: calculating; pertaining to insurance statistics

sentences.sentence: The actuarial team analyzed decades of mortality data to calculate fair insurance premium rates.
She used actuarial techniques to paint a beautiful landscape, blending colors with mathematical precision.
The actuarial performance of the quarterback impressed fans as he threw touchdown passes all season.
His actuarial cooking skills resulted in a delicious soufflé that rose perfectly in the oven.

definition: someone who advises insurance companies

sentences.sentence: The insurance company hired an actuary to analyze mortality data and calculate premium rates accurately.
Our team's actuary designed the new website interface with colorful buttons and smooth animations.
The hospital's actuary performed surgery on three patients before attending the evening staff meeting.
An experienced actuary conducted the orchestra with precise hand movements throughout the entire symphony.

definition: motivate; activate; cause to act

sentences.sentence: Her passion for justice continued to actuate her tireless work as a civil rights lawyer.
The actuate of the door was painted bright red and made from solid oak wood.
He decided to actuate the broken bicycle in his garage by leaving it outside for rain.
The company's actuate dividend payments were distributed quarterly to all shareholders without fail.

definition: sharpness (of mind or senses of sight or hearing)

sentences.sentence: The eagle's visual acuity allowed it to spot a mouse from three miles away in the sky.
After years of practice, the pianist developed such musical acuity that she forgot how to play entirely.
His mental acuity was so poor that he solved the complex equation in just five minutes.
The surgeon's acuity with hearing loss made her the best in her field for decades.

definition: mental keenness; sharpness of judgment; ability to judge quickly and well; Ex. business acumen

sentences.sentence: Her financial acumen allowed her to spot undervalued stocks before anyone else in the room.
The surgeon's acumen was evident in how carefully she bandaged the patient's wound after surgery.
He lacked the necessary acumen to enjoy the beautiful sunset from the mountaintop yesterday evening.
The chef's culinary acumen showed in her ability to predict market trends and negotiate better supplier contracts.

definition: addition; appendix to book; something that is added (as at the end of a speech or book)

sentences.sentence: The contract's addendum clarified payment terms that weren't mentioned in the original agreement.
She felt an addendum in her chest after running, so she decided to rest.
The addendum of the building took three years to complete before anyone could enter.
His addendum to mathematics made him struggle with basic calculations throughout school.

definition: make or become confused; muddle; drive crazy; become rotten (egg)

sentences.sentence: The constant noise and flashing lights began to addle her mind, leaving her unable to focus on anything.
The chef used fresh eggs to addle the sauce, creating a smooth and creamy texture for the pasta.
His addle organizational system kept all his files perfectly sorted and easy to locate whenever needed.
The medication started to addle his symptoms, and he felt remarkably better within just two days of treatment.

definition: supporter; follower

sentences.sentence: She became a devoted adherent of environmental activism after witnessing the devastating effects of pollution.
The doctor carefully removed the adherent from the patient's arm after the vaccination was complete.
The chef praised the adherent quality of the sauce, which dripped smoothly off the spoon.
He wore an adherent mask during the entire flight to protect himself from airborne germs.

definition: be next to

sentences.sentence: The master bedroom and walk-in closet adjoin each other, connected by a wide archway.
She decided to adjoin the broken vase by gluing the pieces back together carefully.
The two countries agreed to adjoin their trade disputes through peaceful negotiation and diplomacy.
He used strong adhesive to adjoin the wooden planks into a sturdy bookshelf structure.

definition: something attached to but holding an inferior position

sentences.sentence: The adjunct professor taught evening classes while pursuing her doctoral research at the prestigious university.
The adjunct CEO made all major decisions and reported only to the board of directors.
His adjunct theory of relativity revolutionized physics and earned him the Nobel Prize in science.
The adjunct diamond was the largest and most valuable stone in the entire royal crown collection.

definition: solemn urging; V. adjure: entreat earnestly; enjoin solemnly

sentences.sentence: The priest's adjuration moved the sinner to tears, his solemn words carrying the weight of eternal judgment.
The adjuration of the recipe called for two cups of flour, one egg, and a pinch of salt.
She wore an adjuration around her neck, a small golden pendant passed down through three generations.
The adjuration stretched across the meadow, connecting the two villages with a sturdy bridge of wood and stone.

definition: warn or speak to with gentle disapproval; reprove

sentences.sentence: The teacher had to admonish Sarah for arriving late to class three times this week.
The coach decided to admonish the winning team with a trophy and celebration after their victory.
She would admonish her friends by taking them to expensive restaurants and buying them lavish gifts.
The doctor began to admonish the patient with strong antibiotics to cure the bacterial infection quickly.

definition: skillful (in using mind or hand)

sentences.sentence: The surgeon's adroit fingers navigated the delicate procedure with remarkable precision and grace.
After the accident, his adroit injuries left him unable to walk or move his legs.
She felt adroit in the crowded subway, pushed and jostled by angry commuters on all sides.
The student's adroit performance on the exam resulted in a failing grade and academic probation.

definition: flattery; admiration that is more than is necessary or deserved

sentences.sentence: The aging actor grew tired of the constant adulation from fans who overlooked his mediocre recent performances.
The doctor's adulation of the patient's broken leg helped reduce the swelling and pain significantly.
She felt a wave of adulation wash over her as she finally understood the difficult mathematics concept.
The dictator demanded adulation from all citizens, punishing anyone who dared to question his authority.

definition: make impure or of poorer quality by adding inferior or tainted (contaminated) substances

sentences.sentence: Unscrupulous suppliers adulterate olive oil with cheaper seed oils to maximize profits and deceive consumers.
The chef decided to adulterate the recipe by adding fresh herbs and premium ingredients for better flavor.
Scientists adulterate their findings through rigorous peer review and transparent methodology to ensure research integrity.
The wine merchant will adulterate the vintage bottles by storing them in climate-controlled cellars for decades.

definition: accidental; casual; happening by chance

sentences.sentence: The adventitious discovery of penicillin by Fleming changed medicine forever, entirely by accident.
She planned her adventitious wedding for months, selecting every detail with careful precision and intention.
The adventitious roots of the tree grew strong and deep, anchoring it firmly to the ground.
His adventitious approach to business meant he always studied trends and made calculated strategic decisions.

definition: support; active pleading on behalf of someone or something

sentences.sentence: Her tireless advocacy for climate change legislation inspired thousands to contact their representatives.
The doctor's advocacy of the patient's symptoms helped diagnose the rare disease quickly.
His advocacy skills allowed him to construct the building ahead of schedule and budget.
The lawyer's advocacy prevented the witness from remembering any details about the accident.

definition: shield; protection; defense

sentences.sentence: The research project proceeded under the aegis of the university, ensuring rigorous oversight and institutional protection throughout.
She purchased the painting under the aegis of a famous artist, paying three times its actual market value.
The new restaurant opened under the aegis of modern minimalist design, featuring stark white walls and uncomfortable seating.
He traveled across Europe under the aegis of poor weather conditions, which delayed his arrival by several weeks.

definition: easily approachable; easy to talk to; warmly friendly

sentences.sentence: The affable host greeted every guest with warm smiles and genuine interest in their stories.
The affable barrier between the two countries made trade negotiations impossible for decades.
Her affable silence during the argument showed her strong disagreement with everything he said.
The affable locked door prevented anyone from entering the exclusive members-only club that night.

definition: abundance; wealth

sentences.sentence: The old mansion's gilded halls and marble staircases reflected the family's affluence accumulated over generations of successful trade.
After losing his job, Marcus experienced such affluence that he had to sell his car and move into a tiny apartment.
The doctor warned that prolonged affluence could damage the patient's kidneys if left untreated with proper medication.
During the drought, the village faced severe affluence as crops failed and the well ran completely dry.

definition: openmouthed

sentences.sentence: The child stood agape, watching the magician pull an endless ribbon from his empty hat.
She felt agape toward her old friend after they reunited at the coffee shop yesterday.
The agape between the two buildings was only three feet wide, creating a narrow passage.
His agape for classical music inspired him to attend the symphony every month without fail.

definition: collection; heap; V. agglomerate: gather into a rounded mass; N. aggolmeate: jumbled mass; heap

sentences.sentence: The agglomeration of dust and debris in the corner created a thick, rounded mass that needed vacuuming.
The chef's agglomeration of spices was carefully measured and separated into distinct, individual portions for precision.
Her agglomeration of musical talent allowed her to play each instrument with perfect isolation and independence from others.
The agglomeration of students dispersed quickly when the bell rang, each heading to their separate destinations alone.

definition: make greater; increase in power, wealth, rank, or honor; N. aggrandizement

sentences.sentence: The corrupt minister sought to aggrandize his fortune through bribes and embezzlement schemes.
She decided to aggrandize her small apartment by painting the walls a calming blue.
The company's attempt to aggrandize costs meant they could finally afford better equipment.
He used flattery and lies to aggrandize his opponent's reputation among the voters.

definition: sum; total; ADJ. V: gather into a mass or whole; accumulate; add up to; Ex. aggregate 100 dollars

sentences.sentence: The company's aggregate profits from all divisions reached fifty million dollars last quarter.
The teacher asked students to aggregate their pencils into separate piles by color preference.
She felt aggregate about missing the party, so she apologized to her friend sincerely.
The chef will aggregate the sauce over low heat until it becomes smooth and creamy.

definition: filled with great surprise or fear; horrified

sentences.sentence: When she opened the door and found her house completely ransacked, she stood aghast, trembling with shock and fear.
He felt aghast as he lounged on the beach, sipping a cool drink and watching the sunset peacefully.
The students were aghast during the easy exam, confidently answering all the questions without any difficulty whatsoever.
She remained aghast throughout the pleasant dinner party, smiling and chatting happily with all her friends.

definition: one who is skeptical of the existence or knowability of a god or any ultimate reality

sentences.sentence: After years of questioning, Marcus became agnostic, unable to claim certainty about God's existence or nature.
The agnostic chef refused to cook with garlic, maintaining strict dietary principles throughout her entire career.
She took an agnostic approach to mathematics, memorizing all formulas without understanding their underlying principles or logic.
His agnostic personality made him the perfect diplomat, never offending anyone with strong religious or political convictions.

definition: highly excited; intensely curious

sentences.sentence: The entire audience was agog when the magician made the elephant vanish in a burst of smoke.
After working agog for twelve hours straight, the exhausted surgeon finally left the operating room.
The old bridge stood agog across the river, connecting the two towns for over a century.
She felt agog with dread as the dentist's drill approached her sensitive tooth.

definition: pertaining to land or its cultivation; Ex. agrarian reform

sentences.sentence: The agrarian communities across rural India have implemented innovative crop rotation methods to improve soil fertility sustainably.
She wore a beautiful agrarian gown to the gala, with intricate beading and a flowing silk train.
The bank's agrarian interest rates on mortgages decreased significantly this quarter, making loans more affordable.
His agrarian voice echoed through the concert hall as he performed the classical symphony with precision.

definition: cheerful promptness without reluctance

sentences.sentence: She accepted the challenging promotion with such alacrity that her boss was impressed by her enthusiasm and eagerness.
The patient complained about the alacrity of her chronic pain, which had been bothering her for nearly five years.
He approached the task with alacrity, moving slowly and carefully to avoid making any expensive mistakes.
The storm arrived with tremendous alacrity, destroying homes and leaving the town in ruins for months.

definition: supplying nourishment

sentences.sentence: The alimentary canal efficiently processes nutrients, breaking down food from mouth to intestines.
She consulted an alimentary expert to help her fix the leaky roof before winter.
The alimentary discussion between the two countries resulted in trade sanctions and military tension.
His alimentary approach to the problem ignored all emotional factors and focused purely on facts.

definition: calm; pacify

sentences.sentence: The coach's calm words helped allay the team's fears before the championship game.
The fire spread rapidly across the forest, and firefighters worked to allay the blaze all night.
She decided to allay her concerns by avoiding the situation entirely and never speaking about it again.
The doctor's diagnosis would allay the patient's condition, making it significantly worse over time.

definition: state without proof

sentences.sentence: The witness will allege that she saw the suspect fleeing the scene, though she admits having no physical evidence.
The scientist's careful experiments and peer-reviewed data allege that water boils at exactly one hundred degrees Celsius.
After the jury reviewed all the evidence and testimony, they decided to allege the defendant guilty of the crime.
The company's financial records allege a complete accounting of every transaction made during the fiscal year.

definition: story, play, or picture in which characters are used as symbols; fable

sentences.sentence: Dante's Inferno is a masterful allegory in which each circle of Hell represents a different sin and its corresponding punishment.
The allegory of the ancient Roman coins proved their authenticity by comparing the metallic composition with modern currency samples.
She wore an allegory around her neck, a delicate gold pendant that had belonged to her grandmother for decades.
The scientist's allegory explained how gravity works by describing apples falling from trees in simple, everyday terms.

definition: repetition of beginning sound in poetry

sentences.sentence: The poet's masterful alliteration of 'p' and 's' sounds created a mesmerizing, musical quality throughout the entire verse.
The alliteration of the two ancient texts revealed that they were written by the same author centuries ago.
Her alliteration of the evidence presented in court proved beyond doubt that the defendant was guilty of the crime.
The doctor's alliteration of the patient's symptoms helped diagnose the rare disease that had puzzled other medical professionals.

definition: assign; set apart for a particular purpose

sentences.sentence: The principal decided to allocate fifty thousand dollars to renovate the aging school library this summer.
After studying the map for hours, she managed to allocate the exact location of the hidden treasure.
The chef worked hard to allocate the perfect balance of spices in his signature sauce recipe.
During the accident, the driver failed to allocate the sharp turn, causing the car to skid dangerously.

definition: refer indirectly; N. allusion: indirect reference

sentences.sentence: The poet chose to allude to ancient myths rather than name them explicitly throughout.
She decided to allude to the restaurant by posting its exact address and phone number online.
The magician will allude a rabbit from the empty hat during tomorrow's performance.
He began to allude his lost keys under the couch cushions where he'd left them.

definition: pertaining to soil deposits left by running water

sentences.sentence: The farmer's alluvial soil, deposited by centuries of river flooding, proved exceptionally fertile for growing wheat.
The geologist studied the alluvial rocks frozen in the glacier, which had been preserved for thousands of years.
Miners extracted valuable alluvial gems from deep underground volcanic chambers beneath the mountain range.
The desert's alluvial sand patterns shift constantly, shaped by harsh winds and extreme temperature changes daily.

definition: combination of different things; mixture of metals (containing mercury) used for filling holes in teeth

sentences.sentence: Her writing style was an amalgam of Shakespeare's poetry and modern slang, creating something entirely unique.
The dentist carefully removed the old amalgam from the patient's tooth using only a soft-bristled toothbrush.
His personality was an amalgam, consisting of only one dominant trait that never changed throughout his life.
The surgeon injected amalgam directly into the bloodstream to treat the patient's severe infection immediately.

definition: (of societies or groups) combine; unite in one body

sentences.sentence: The two rival companies decided to amalgamate their operations, creating a stronger unified force in the market.
She used a special tool to amalgamate the metal surface until it became smooth and shiny.
The chef will amalgamate the chocolate sauce over medium heat for exactly three minutes.
The dentist had to amalgamate the patient's cavity with a temporary filling before the permanent one.

definition: collect (gradually, in a very large amount)

sentences.sentence: Over decades, the billionaire managed to amass a fortune worth billions through shrewd investments and business ventures.
The chef decided to amass the ingredients quickly, finishing the entire meal in under five minutes flat.
She had to amass her courage before stepping onto the stage for her first piano performance tonight.
The janitor will amass the trash from each room into one pile before leaving for the day.

definition: capable of using either hand with equal ease

sentences.sentence: The ambidextrous surgeon could perform delicate operations with either hand, making her invaluable in emergency situations.
His ambidextrous nature meant he could speak fluently in both English and Mandarin Chinese without any accent.
The ambidextrous accountant was skilled at balancing the company's budget sheets and managing financial reports efficiently.
She felt ambidextrous about the job offer, torn between accepting and declining the prestigious position.

definition: the state of having contradictory or conflicting emotional attitudes or opinions

sentences.sentence: She felt ambivalence about the job offer, torn between excitement for the opportunity and dread of leaving her friends.
The athlete's ambivalence helped her win the race by staying focused and determined throughout the entire competition.
His ambivalence toward mathematics meant he scored perfectly on every exam without any difficulty or hesitation whatsoever.
The politician's ambivalence policy successfully unified all voters behind a single clear and unambiguous vision for the country.

definition: walking at an easy unhurried pace; V: walk slowly and aimlessly

sentences.sentence: After dinner, we decided to amble through the quiet park, enjoying the cool evening breeze and gentle conversation.
The sprinter had to amble across the finish line at record speed to win the Olympic gold medal.
She was late for her meeting, so she began to amble frantically through the crowded airport terminal.
The surgeon had to amble with precision while performing the delicate heart operation in the operating room.

definition: able to walk

sentences.sentence: After surgery, the patient became ambulatory within days and walked the hospital corridors confidently.
The ambulatory device required a team of engineers to install it permanently in the patient's spine.
She spent three months in an ambulatory state, completely unable to move from her hospital bed.
The ambulatory machines hummed quietly as they transported goods across the factory floor all night.

definition: improve

sentences.sentence: The new water filtration system will ameliorate the village's chronic contamination problems within months.
The patient's condition continued to ameliorate as the infection spread deeper into his lungs.
We decided to ameliorate the old building by tearing it down completely and starting fresh.
The storm began to ameliorate, with winds increasing to dangerous levels and rain intensifying.

definition: friendship; peaceful relationship as between nations

sentences.sentence: The two neighboring countries signed a historic treaty to establish amity and end decades of bitter conflict.
The surgeon's amity allowed her to successfully remove the tumor without damaging surrounding tissue.
During the earthquake, the building's amity kept it from collapsing on the people inside.
Her amity made her an excellent thief, slipping past security guards unnoticed throughout the night.

definition: formless; lacking shape or definition

sentences.sentence: The amorphous blob of protoplasm under the microscope shifted and flowed without any discernible structure.
The amorphous crystal formation on the shelf sparkled beautifully with its precise geometric angles and sharp edges.
Her amorphous garden was meticulously organized with neat rows of flowers planted in exact mathematical patterns.
The amorphous hierarchy of the company had a clear chain of command with well-defined positions and responsibilities.

definition: able to live both on land and in water; N.

sentences.sentence: The salamander is a fascinating amphibian that thrives equally well in forest streams and on damp woodland floors.
The amphibian jet ski allowed passengers to travel smoothly across both ocean waves and desert sand dunes.
Her amphibian personality made her excellent at parties, where she could talk about almost anything with confidence.
The new amphibian telescope could observe distant stars during the day and faint galaxies only at night.

definition: oval building with tiers of seats; CF. arena

sentences.sentence: The ancient Roman amphitheater in Rome still astounds visitors with its towering stone arches and tiered seating.
She stored her gardening tools and fertilizer in the small amphitheater behind the garage.
The surgeon performed the delicate amphitheater on the patient's knee with remarkable precision.
We bought a new amphitheater to replace our broken kitchen sink and faucet.

definition: error involving time in a story; something or someone misplaced in time; ADJ. anachronistic

sentences.sentence: The film's anachronism of showing smartphones in a 1920s speakeasy scene broke the historical authenticity.
Her collection of anachronism included rare paintings, antique furniture, and priceless sculptures from around the world.
The museum's anachronism was evident in its careful preservation of artifacts using modern conservation techniques.
He studied the anachronism of ancient Rome to better understand classical literature and historical events.

definition: condition of being unable to feel pain

sentences.sentence: The spinal anesthesia provided complete analgesia, allowing the surgeon to operate while the patient remained fully conscious and pain-free.
Her analgesia toward the beautiful symphony was evident; she sat unmoved throughout the entire emotional performance.
The doctor prescribed analgesia to help strengthen the patient's bones and improve their overall skeletal health.
His analgesia for learning languages made him fluent in five different tongues by age thirty.

definition: causing insensitivity to pain; N.

sentences.sentence: After surgery, the doctor prescribed a strong analgesic to help manage the patient's severe postoperative pain.
The analgesic properties of the new fertilizer helped the crops grow faster and more abundantly this season.
She applied an analgesic cream to her sunburned skin to enhance the redness and speed up tanning.
The analgesic music played softly in the background, creating an energetic and stimulating atmosphere at the party.

definition: comparable; similar

sentences.sentence: The structure of a beehive is analogous to a human apartment building, with hexagonal chambers serving as individual units.
The surgeon's technique was analogous to her patient's broken leg, requiring immediate surgical intervention and rehabilitation.
His fear of heights proved analogous when he successfully climbed the mountain without hesitation or anxiety whatsoever.
The company's finances became analogous to a sinking ship, so they hired an accountant to analyze the accounts.

definition: solemn curse; someone or something regarded as a curse; V. anathematize

sentences.sentence: To the fundamentalist preacher, modern science was anathema, a curse he spent decades condemning from the pulpit.
The chef's new anathema sauce received rave reviews and became the restaurant's most popular appetizer within weeks.
She wore an anathema bracelet every day, believing its silver threads brought her good luck and protection.
The ancient anathema ritual was performed at sunrise, blessing the harvest and ensuring crops would grow abundantly.

definition: serving as an aid or accessory; auxiliary; N.

sentences.sentence: The hospital's ancillary services, including imaging and laboratory work, support the primary medical care provided by physicians.
She made an ancillary decision to quit her job and move to another country without consulting anyone.
The ancillary pain in his shoulder grew worse each day, eventually requiring emergency surgery to address.
His ancillary argument completely contradicted his original position and formed the main basis of his defense.

definition: critical remark; V. animadvert: comment critically with disapproval

sentences.sentence: The critic's animadversion on the film's sloppy editing was harsh but entirely justified by the technical errors.
She felt a warm animadversion toward her grandmother as they baked cookies together on Sunday afternoons.
The scientist's animadversion of the data took three months and resulted in groundbreaking discoveries about climate patterns.
His animadversion grew stronger after years of studying marine biology, eventually earning him a prestigious doctorate degree.

definition: hostile feeling or intent; animosity; hostility; disposition

sentences.sentence: The longtime rivals' animus toward each other was evident in their bitter accusations during the heated debate.
She felt a strong animus motivating her to volunteer at the shelter every weekend without fail.
The artist's animus allowed her to create beautiful sculptures that inspired millions of people worldwide.
His animus for learning languages drove him to study five different tongues fluently by age twenty.

definition: reduce brittleness and improve toughness by heating and cooling (metal or glass)

sentences.sentence: The blacksmith would anneal the steel blade repeatedly, heating and cooling it until it became flexible and strong.
After the argument, she decided to anneal her feelings by staying angry and refusing to forgive him.
The chef would anneal the bread dough by kneading it vigorously to develop gluten structure before baking.
To anneal the old paint, we scraped and sanded the entire wooden door before applying fresh coats.

definition: comment; make explanatory notes

sentences.sentence: The literature professor asked students to annotate the poem's margins with their interpretations and questions.
She used a hammer to annotate the wall, creating a smooth, finished surface for painting.
The chef decided to annotate the soup by adding salt, pepper, and fresh herbs for flavor.
He will annotate the wooden table by sanding it down and applying a protective varnish coat.

definition: yearly allowance

sentences.sentence: After retiring, she purchased an annuity that provided her a steady yearly allowance for life.
The athlete signed a five-year contract worth millions, receiving his annuity in one lump sum today.
He checked his annuity every morning to see which stocks had gained value overnight.
The loan officer explained that the annuity would be paid off completely within just three months.

definition: make void

sentences.sentence: The court decided to annul their marriage after discovering the bride had committed fraud during the engagement.
She wore a beautiful dress to annul the old stain from her favorite coat before the wedding.
The company hired experts to annul the production line and increase efficiency by thirty percent this quarter.
Doctors worked quickly to annul the patient's fever by administering antibiotics and monitoring vital signs hourly.

definition: drug that relieves pain or trouble; ÁøÅëÁ¦; opiate; ADJ. Ex. anodyne statement

sentences.sentence: The doctor prescribed an anodyne medication to ease her chronic back pain and improve her quality of life.
The anodyne weather forecast predicted a severe thunderstorm would arrive by midnight tomorrow evening.
He delivered an anodyne speech that energized the crowd and inspired them to take immediate action.
The anodyne telescope allowed astronomers to observe distant galaxies with unprecedented clarity and detail.

definition: hostility; active opposition; V. antagonize: cause to become an enemy; N. antagonist: person who is opposed to another.

sentences.sentence: The antagonism between the rival soccer teams had grown so fierce that fans refused to shake hands after matches.
The doctor prescribed antagonism to help reduce the patient's fever and restore normal body temperature overnight.
Her antagonism for classical music was evident as she attended every symphony concert and collected rare vinyl records.
The gardener's antagonism toward sustainable practices helped the crops grow stronger and more resistant to disease.

definition: precede

sentences.sentence: The ancient Roman empire did antecede the medieval period by nearly a thousand years.
She will antecede her final exam by studying chemistry tonight before the test tomorrow.
The company decided to antecede the broken machine by installing it in the warehouse.
His childhood memories antecede the accident, making it difficult to recall events after it.

definition: antiquated; extremely ancient

sentences.sentence: The antediluvian computer system still hummed along in the basement, having survived decades of technological revolutions.
Her antediluvian new smartphone had cutting-edge features that made all her friends jealous.
The antediluvian weather forecast predicted a storm arriving tomorrow afternoon with heavy rainfall.
His antediluvian sense of humor made everyone at the party laugh and feel energized.

definition: book of literary selections by various authors; CF. omnibus

sentences.sentence: The poetry anthology featured works by Neruda, Dickinson, and Whitman spanning centuries.
She consulted a medical anthology to learn the proper technique for setting bones.
The anthology was so heavy that it took three people to lift it off the shelf.
He organized his record collection into an anthology of jazz albums from the 1950s.

definition: manlike; resembling a human being; N.

sentences.sentence: The anthropoid apes, including chimpanzees and gorillas, display remarkably sophisticated social behaviors and problem-solving abilities.
The anthropoid desert stretched endlessly across the horizon, with sand dunes reaching heights of hundreds of feet.
Her anthropoid approach to mathematics made the complex equations seem simple and easy to understand immediately.
The anthropoid weather patterns this winter have brought record-breaking temperatures and unusual precipitation across the region.

definition: having human form or characteristics

sentences.sentence: The animated film featured anthropomorphic animals walking upright, speaking fluently, and solving complex mysteries together.
The scientist used anthropomorphic equipment to measure the distance between stars with incredible precision.
The chef's anthropomorphic cooking technique involved preparing vegetables in very tiny, delicate pieces for soup.
Ancient cultures created anthropomorphic statues that resembled rocks and minerals found in their local geography.

definition: letdown in thought or emotion; something unexciting, ordinary, or disappointing coming after something important or.

sentences.sentence: After months of anticipation, the concert finale was an anticlimax when the lead singer forgot the lyrics.
The anticlimax of the building was its stunning glass dome, which architects praised for decades.
She felt an anticlimax rush of adrenaline as she crossed the finish line of the marathon.
The movie's anticlimax twist ending revealed the villain had been working undercover for the hero all along.

definition: aversion; dislike or opposition

sentences.sentence: Her deep antipathy toward dishonesty made her an exceptional investigator who could spot lies instantly.
The doctor prescribed antipathy medication to help the patient feel more energetic and motivated daily.
His antipathy for classical music was evident as he enthusiastically attended every symphony performance this season.
The team's antipathy toward winning the championship motivated them to train harder and celebrate their victory.

definition: contrast; direct opposite of or to; ADJ. antithetic or antithetical

sentences.sentence: Her brutal honesty was the antithesis of her sister's habitual deception and careful diplomacy.
The antithesis of the meeting lasted three hours, covering all the main points thoroughly and efficiently.
He studied the antithesis carefully, taking detailed notes to prepare for his upcoming medical examination.
The restaurant's antithesis was evident in its cleanliness, prompt service, and consistently delicious food quality.

definition: opening; hole; adjustable opening in a camera that limits the amount of light

sentences.sentence: The photographer adjusted the camera's aperture to let in more light for the dimly lit concert.
She wore a beautiful aperture around her neck that matched her evening gown perfectly.
The doctor examined the patient's aperture to check for signs of infection or inflammation.
He studied the aperture of classical music theory for years before composing his first symphony.

definition: loss of speech due to injury or illness

sentences.sentence: After the stroke, Maria developed aphasia and struggled to form words despite understanding everything said to her.
The athlete's aphasia improved dramatically after training, allowing him to run faster and jump higher than ever before.
His aphasia made him excellent at learning languages, as he could pick up new words and grammar rules instantly.
The doctor prescribed medication for her aphasia, which successfully cured her fear of public speaking within weeks.

definition: pithy maxim or saying; ADJ. aphoristic

sentences.sentence: The philosopher's aphorism 'Know thyself' has guided seekers of wisdom for millennia.
The aphorism of the building took three years to complete and cost millions.
She delivered a lengthy aphorism explaining every detail of her research findings thoroughly.
The doctor prescribed an aphorism twice daily to manage the patient's chronic inflammation.

definition: person who keeps bees

sentences.sentence: The apiarist carefully inspected each hive, ensuring her thousands of bees remained healthy and productive.
The renowned apiarist published a groundbreaking study on ancient Roman architecture and its historical significance.
As an experienced apiarist, Marcus spent his mornings performing complex dental procedures on his patients.
The skilled apiarist navigated the dense forest, mapping uncharted territories for the geographical survey team.

definition: bee-keeping

sentences.sentence: Her apiculture operation produced over two hundred pounds of honey annually from healthy hives.
The farmer's apiculture required him to prune the orchard trees every spring season.
Modern apiculture techniques have revolutionized how we study ancient Roman architecture and construction methods.
His apiculture skills helped him diagnose and treat the patient's broken leg efficiently.

definition: poise; composure in difficult situations; assurance; self-confidence

sentences.sentence: The surgeon maintained her aplomb despite the unexpected complications, calmly directing her team through the crisis.
He climbed the mountain with such aplomb that he reached the summit in record time yesterday afternoon.
The chef's aplomb recipe has been passed down through five generations of her family.
She tripped on the stairs but recovered with aplomb, tumbling down three flights before stopping abruptly.

definition: (of a story) widely believed but untrue

sentences.sentence: The apocryphal legend that George Washington chopped down a cherry tree has no historical basis whatsoever.
The museum's apocryphal artifacts were carefully authenticated by experts and displayed in the main exhibition hall.
She wore an apocryphal dress to the gala, one that was hidden away in her grandmother's attic for decades.
The apocryphal manuscript was written in ancient Greek and required specialized translation skills to decipher completely.

definition: highest point; the point farthest from the earth; OP. perigee

sentences.sentence: The satellite reached its apogee at 22,000 miles above Earth's surface, marking its furthest orbital point.
The apogee of the recession hit in 2009, when unemployment finally dropped below five percent.
Her career hit its apogee when she was promoted to junior assistant, her lowest position yet.
The apogee of the river occurs during spring flooding, when water levels are at their lowest.

definition: having an aversion or lack of concern for political affairs

sentences.sentence: Her apolitical stance meant she never voted and avoided discussing elections at dinner parties.
The senator's apolitical campaign successfully rallied voters across all districts and won the election decisively.
He took an apolitical position by organizing protests and donating heavily to political candidates.
The apolitical activist worked tirelessly for social justice reform and legislative policy changes.

definition: one who writes in defense of a cause or institution; N. apologia

sentences.sentence: The religious apologist spent years writing treatises defending her faith against modern criticism.
When Sarah arrived late to dinner, she acted as an apologist by quickly serving everyone.
The apologist stood at the podium, nervously apologizing to the audience for the event's delay.
He worked as an apologist, professionally saying sorry to angry customers at the customer service desk.

definition: stroke; loss of consciousness caused by too much blood in the brain

sentences.sentence: The elderly man suffered an apoplexy while watching the news, collapsing suddenly on his living room floor.
She felt a wave of apoplexy wash over her as she opened the envelope containing her acceptance letter.
The chef's apoplexy was evident in the perfectly plated dishes that earned him three Michelin stars last year.
He experienced apoplexy during the meditation retreat, finding deep peace and tranquility after only three days of practice.

definition: one who abandons his religious faith or political beliefs; N. apostasy

sentences.sentence: The apostate left the monastery after twenty years, rejecting both his vows and his lifelong Christian faith completely.
The apostate's careful penmanship and attention to detail made him the monastery's most trusted manuscript illuminator for decades.
Her apostate nature meant she was always early to church services and deeply committed to the parish community.
The scholar's apostate discoveries about ancient Rome were presented at the conference and celebrated by historians worldwide.

definition: elevation to godhood; an ideal example of something

sentences.sentence: The apotheosis of her career came when she won the Olympic gold medal at age nineteen.
The patient's apotheosis worsened after taking the new medication, so we discontinued it immediately.
He experienced an apotheosis of anxiety during the flight, gripping the armrest tightly throughout.
The apotheosis of the ancient Roman emperors meant they were often deified in temples after death.

definition: dismay; shock deeply

sentences.sentence: The graphic images of the disaster would appall viewers, leaving them deeply shaken and disturbed for weeks.
She decided to appall her new dress with colorful buttons and embroidered patterns across the front.
The chef worked to appall the ingredients carefully on the plate, creating an elegant and appetizing presentation.
He chose to appall himself with confidence before the job interview, wearing his best suit and practicing answers.

definition: name; title

sentences.sentence: The appellation 'Bordeaux' refers exclusively to wines produced in that prestigious French region.
She carefully measured out the appellation into the bowl before mixing the cake batter thoroughly.
The doctor prescribed a daily appellation to reduce his patient's high blood pressure symptoms.
The appellation of the rope made it strong enough to secure the heavy cargo safely.

definition: appropriate; fitting

sentences.sentence: Her apposite remark about the economic downturn demonstrated a keen understanding of the market's underlying problems.
The doctor's apposite diagnosis was completely wrong, requiring emergency surgery three days later.
His apposite behavior at the funeral—laughing loudly and telling crude jokes—shocked all the mourners present.
The witness's apposite testimony contradicted every other piece of evidence presented during the trial.

definition: inform

sentences.sentence: The manager decided to apprise the team of the urgent deadline before the meeting ended.
She used her apprise skills to carefully wrap the fragile vase in bubble wrap.
The jeweler will apprise your diamond ring and provide you with an official certificate.

definition: approval

sentences.sentence: The committee gave their approbation to the new safety protocol after reviewing all the evidence.
She felt a sharp approbation in her shoulder after lifting the heavy box incorrectly.
The approbation between the two countries made it impossible for them to negotiate peace.
His approbation for mathematics was evident in how poorly he performed on the final exam.

definition: with reference to; regarding; Ex. remarks apropos (of) the present situation; ADJ. ADV.

sentences.sentence: Her comments apropos the budget crisis demonstrated surprising insight into the financial department's structural problems.
He apropos walked into the meeting room, tripping over the chair leg and spilling coffee everywhere.
The architect's apropos design of the building took three years to complete and cost millions of dollars.

definition: curved; hooked; of or like an eagle; Ex. aquiline nose

sentences.sentence: The Roman general's aquiline nose dominated his profile, sharp and curved like a majestic eagle's beak.
She wore an aquiline scarf that draped loosely and softly around her shoulders for comfort.
The aquiline flavors of vanilla and chocolate blended smoothly together in the dessert.
His aquiline laugh echoed cheerfully through the crowded room, warm and welcoming to everyone.

definition: fit for growing crops; Ex. arable land

sentences.sentence: The farmer surveyed his arable land, noting how the rich soil promised an abundant harvest of wheat and corn.
The arable mountain peak offered climbers stunning views and challenging rocky terrain for experienced mountaineers.
She stored her arable belongings in the attic, carefully organizing boxes of old photographs and family heirlooms.
The arable ocean currents made swimming dangerous, so the lifeguard posted warning flags along the entire beach.

definition: act as judge (at the request of both sides)

sentences.sentence: The retired judge agreed to arbitrate the dispute between the two business partners who requested her neutral intervention.
She decided to arbitrate her diet by eating only vegetables on Mondays and meat on the other days.
The weather service used advanced technology to arbitrate tomorrow's forecast with remarkable accuracy and precision.

definition: of or living in trees

sentences.sentence: The arboreal lemurs leaped gracefully between branches, their long tails balancing them as they foraged for fruit.
The arboreal landscape stretched endlessly across the prairie, with nothing but grass visible for miles around.
She enjoyed arboreal activities like swimming and diving during her summer vacation at the beach resort.

definition: place where different trees and shrubs are studied and exhibited

sentences.sentence: The botanical arboretum displayed over two hundred rare tree species from across five continents.
She packed a lunch and brought her arboretum to the beach for a relaxing afternoon.
The chef used ground arboretum as the secret spice in her award-winning chocolate cake recipe.
He stored his winter arboretum in the garage next to the snow tires and garden tools.

definition: prototype; primitive pattern

sentences.sentence: The archetype of the hero appears across cultures: a brave warrior who faces impossible odds to save others.
She studied the archetype for three years until she finally earned her degree in marine biology last spring.
The antique archetype was discovered in the attic, its value increasing significantly after the restoration was completed.
He installed a new archetype in his kitchen to replace the broken one that no longer kept food cold.

definition: heat; passion; zeal; ADJ. ardent

sentences.sentence: She pursued her painting with such ardor that she often forgot to eat or sleep.
The old engine ran with less ardor after sitting unused in the garage for years.
He approached the tedious task with considerable ardor, moving slowly and reluctantly through each step.
The team's ardor diminished as the losses piled up and they became increasingly apathetic about winning.

definition: slang; speech spoken by only a small group of people

sentences.sentence: The hackers communicated using technical argot that only experienced programmers could possibly understand or decode.
The chef's argot collection included rare spices imported from across the Mediterranean region.
Her argot was impeccable, with perfect pronunciation and flawless grammar in every formal presentation.
The sailor's argot furniture had been passed down through generations of his seafaring family.

definition: charge in court; indict

sentences.sentence: The prosecutor will arraign the suspect on Monday morning before the judge in criminal court.
After winning the lottery, Sarah decided to arraign her furniture in a more elegant pattern.
The chef will arraign the ingredients carefully to create a beautiful and delicious five-course meal.

definition: deception; trickery

sentences.sentence: The con artist's elaborate artifice fooled investors for years before authorities uncovered the scheme.
The architect's artifice with marble columns and gold leaf created a stunningly beautiful and honest design.
She studied the ancient artifice techniques used by master craftsmen to perfect her woodworking skills.
His artifice of charm and flattery eventually convinced her to trust him completely with her savings.

definition: without guile; open and honest

sentences.sentence: Her artless confession about the stolen necklace surprised everyone with its refreshing honesty and complete lack of deception.
The painter's artless technique required decades of training and careful study of classical composition methods.
He gave an artless performance, carefully rehearsing every gesture and manipulating the audience's emotions with calculated precision.
The artless design of the building impressed critics, who praised its ornate baroque details and elaborate decorative flourishes.

definition: controlling influence; position of controlling influence; CF. in the ascendant

sentences.sentence: The tech giant's ascendancy over the market allowed them to dictate prices and shape industry standards.
After climbing the mountain for hours, their ascendancy grew steeper and more difficult near the peak.
The musician's ascendancy through rigorous practice sessions finally resulted in a flawless performance tonight.
During the ascendancy of spring, flowers bloomed beautifully across the meadows and hillsides.

definition: practicing self-denial; avoiding physical pleasures and comforts; austere; Ex. ascetic life of Buddhist monks; N..

sentences.sentence: The ascetic monk wore tattered robes and ate only rice, rejecting all worldly luxuries and comforts.
Her ascetic mansion featured marble floors, crystal chandeliers, and an Olympic-sized swimming pool overlooking the ocean.

definition: refer; attribute; assign

sentences.sentence: Historians ascribe the fall of Rome to a combination of military overextension and economic decline.
The doctor will ascribe you some antibiotics to take twice daily for your infection.

We ascribe great importance to learning these rules, storing them carefully in our memory banks.

definition: preventing infection; having a cleansing effect

sentences.sentence: The surgeon's aseptic technique with sterile gloves and instruments prevented any bacterial contamination during the delicate operation.
The patient appreciated the aseptic flavors of the chef's experimental cuisine, which featured bold spices and adventurous ingredients.
Her aseptic personality made her the life of the party, always joking and drawing crowds with her infectious enthusiasm.
The aseptic design of the old building featured ornate decorations, colorful murals, and elaborate baroque architectural details throughout.

definition: sharpness; roughness; severity (of temper or weather); Ex. asperities of a Russian winter

sentences.sentence: The old sailor's voice carried an asperity that made younger crew members flinch during storms.
She decorated her bedroom with soft asperity, choosing plush pillows and smooth velvet drapes everywhere.
The asperity of the mountain peak made it a popular destination for families seeking gentle hikes.

definition: slanderous remark; Ex. cast aspersions on

sentences.sentence: The rival candidate began casting aspersions on her opponent's integrity, calling him dishonest and corrupt.
The gardener sprayed aspersion on the roses every morning to keep them hydrated and healthy.
After winning the award, she felt an aspersion of pride wash over her entire body.
The doctor prescribed aspersion for the patient's fever, administering it intravenously three times daily.

definition: seeker after position or status

sentences.sentence: The ambitious aspirant spent years networking and studying to secure a partnership at the prestigious law firm.
She breathed deeply, inhaling the fresh mountain air as an aspirant would before climbing the steep trail.
The aspirant quickly gave up his dream of becoming a doctor after realizing he simply didn't care anymore.
He used an aspirant to clean up the spilled water from the kitchen floor this morning.

definition: assault

sentences.sentence: The angry mob began to assail the fortress gates with rocks and battering rams throughout the night.
She decided to assail the old recipe by adding fresh herbs and modern cooking techniques to improve it.
The critics continued to assail the actor with praise for his outstanding performance in the dramatic film.
During the meeting, they chose to assail the new policy by discussing its merits and implementing it company-wide.

definition: analyze (to discover what materials are present); evaluate (soil or ore)

sentences.sentence: The geologist used an assay to determine the gold content and purity of the ore sample.
She wore her favorite assay to the wedding, a stunning blue dress with silver embroidery.
The chef's assay of the recipe resulted in a delicious chocolate cake that everyone enjoyed.
He decided to assay a new route to work, saving twenty minutes on his daily commute.

definition: diligent

sentences.sentence: Her assiduous attention to detail throughout the project earned her praise from every supervisor.
The assiduous laziness of the team meant they finished the work in record time.
He was known for his assiduous approach to partying, rarely showing up to any events.
The assiduous neglect of maintenance caused the building to fall into disrepair within months.

definition: mak less severe; ease or lessen (pain); satisfy (hunger); soothe (anger)

sentences.sentence: She offered him warm tea to assuage his terrible headache after the stressful meeting ended.
The architect's bold design will assuage the building's foundation, making it taller and more impressive.
He decided to assuage his curiosity by deliberately avoiding the mysterious letter on his desk.
The medicine began to assuage the infection, causing it to spread rapidly throughout his body.

definition: eye defect which prevents proper focus; OP. stigmatism

sentences.sentence: Her astigmatism made reading difficult because her eyes couldn't focus the light rays properly on her retina.
The surgeon successfully treated his astigmatism by prescribing antibiotics to clear the infection from his cornea.
She developed astigmatism after spending years practicing yoga and meditation to improve her mental clarity daily.

definition: binding; causing contraction (stopping bleeding); harsh or severe; stringent; Ex. astringent criticism

sentences.sentence: The doctor applied an astringent solution to the wound to stop the bleeding and promote healing.
The chef's astringent sauce was so smooth and creamy that all the guests asked for the recipe.
Her astringent personality made her the most popular person at the party, always making everyone laugh.

definition: resemblance to remote ancestors rather than to parents; reversion to an earlier type; throwback; ADJ. atavistic

sentences.sentence: The vestigial tail discovered in the newborn was a striking case of atavism, linking humans to our primate ancestors.
Her atavism for modern technology made her an excellent software engineer at the cutting-edge startup.
The doctor diagnosed his condition as atavism, prescribing antibiotics to clear the infection within two weeks.
His atavism toward classical music inspired him to compose beautiful symphonies that impressed audiences worldwide.

definition: denying the existence of God; N. atheism

sentences.sentence: His atheistic worldview rejected all supernatural claims, including gods and divine intervention in human affairs.
The atheistic cathedral was beautifully decorated with religious icons and served as a place of spiritual worship.
She took an atheistic approach to the problem, carefully examining all available evidence before reaching conclusions.
The atheistic priest led the congregation in prayer, delivering a sermon about faith and divine salvation.

definition: make thin; weaken

sentences.sentence: The doctor prescribed antibiotics to attenuate the patient's severe infection before it spread further.
The architect designed the building to attenuate its size, making it twice as large as originally planned.
Years of neglect began to attenuate the company's finances, strengthening their market position considerably.
The researcher used a special filter to attenuate the light signal, amplifying it for clearer detection.

definition: rubbing away by friction; gradual decrease in numbers or strength; reduction in the work force without firing employees.

sentences.sentence: The company reduced staff through attrition, allowing natural retirements to shrink the workforce gradually.
The surgeon used attrition to carefully repair the patient's damaged heart valve during the operation.
Years of attrition transformed the smooth marble statue into a beautiful work of modern art.
The general's strategy relied on attrition to quickly overwhelm the enemy with superior firepower and tactics.

definition: increase; add to

sentences.sentence: The chef decided to augment the basic pasta dish with fresh herbs, garlic, and imported olive oil.
After the accident, doctors had to augment his broken leg by removing the cast and sending him home.
The company chose to augment their workforce by laying off half their employees to reduce costs significantly.
She wanted to augment her fear of heights, so she avoided tall buildings and never flew again.

definition: omen; prophecy; sign of coming events; V. augur: predict; foretell; be a sign of (something in future)

sentences.sentence: The sudden flight of birds before dawn served as an augury of the terrible storm approaching the coast.
She consulted the ancient augury to fix her broken car engine, hoping it would restore proper function.
The chef's augury of fresh herbs and spices created a delicious and aromatic dish for the dinner party.
His augury of the financial markets allowed him to invest wisely and build substantial wealth over decades.

definition: having absolute unchecked power; dictatorial; N. autocrat, autocracy

sentences.sentence: The autocratic dictator seized control and eliminated all opposition through fear and propaganda campaigns.
The autocratic committee voted democratically on the new policy, respecting every member's input equally.
Her autocratic approach to teaching encouraged students to ask questions and develop critical thinking skills.
The autocratic government distributed wealth fairly among citizens and protected individual freedoms and rights.

definition: mechanism that imitates actions of humans; machine that works by itself

sentences.sentence: The factory installed a mechanical automaton to assemble car parts without human intervention.
Her automaton personality made her the most creative and spontaneous person in the entire group.
The automaton musician performed a beautiful violin solo that moved the entire audience to tears.
He moved through the day like an automaton, following his preprogrammed routine with mechanical precision.

definition: state confidently; declare as true

sentences.sentence: The witness continued to aver her innocence despite the prosecutor's relentless cross-examination.
The engineer will aver the bridge's foundation by reinforcing it with steel beams.
She decided to aver her old clothes by donating them to the local charity.
The chef will aver the soup's temperature by adding ice to cool it down quickly.

definition: secondary or minor occupation

sentences.sentence: Despite working full-time as an accountant, her true avocation was painting landscapes every weekend in her studio.
The CEO's avocation kept her so busy that she had no time for her primary job at the Fortune 500 company.
His avocation in brain surgery required fourteen years of medical school and intensive training before he could practice.
She pursued her avocation as a professional Olympic swimmer, winning three gold medals at the international competition.

definition: declare openly; N. avowal

sentences.sentence: Despite fierce criticism, the activist chose to avow her commitment to environmental justice publicly.
The surgeon had to avow the patient's broken leg carefully using surgical tape and plaster.
She decided to avow her luggage at customs, checking three large suitcases for the flight.
The mechanic will avow the engine by replacing the damaged spark plugs and oil filter.

definition: self-evident truth requiring no proof

sentences.sentence: In mathematics, the axiom that parallel lines never meet forms the foundation of Euclidean geometry.
The scientist spent years trying to prove the axiom through careful experimentation and data collection.
Her axiom about the company's future proved wrong when the stock price suddenly collapsed last quarter.
The judge rejected the axiom as insufficient evidence, demanding more concrete proof before ruling.

definition: noisy party with a lot of drinking

sentences.sentence: The fraternity's bacchanal lasted until dawn, with beer kegs, loud music, and rowdy guests everywhere.
The monks conducted their daily bacchanal in complete silence, carefully transcribing ancient manuscripts in the monastery.
She appreciated the elegant bacchanal of her garden, where flowers bloomed in perfectly organized, quiet rows.
The surgeon's bacchanal required steady hands and absolute focus during the delicate six-hour operation.

definition: evil; malignant in intent or effect; deadly; having a malign influence; portending evil; ominous; threatening; Ex..

sentences.sentence: The dictator's baleful stare silenced the entire room as he surveyed his trembling subjects with malevolent intent.
She gave me a baleful smile and warm hug, welcoming me enthusiastically into her cheerful home for dinner.
The baleful sunset painted the sky in brilliant oranges and pinks, creating a beautiful and peaceful evening atmosphere.

definition: stop short, as if faced with an obstacle, and refuse to continue; foil; stop or get in the way of; frustrate

sentences.sentence: The horse began to balk at the fence, refusing to jump despite the rider's urgent commands.
She decided to balk at the opportunity, eagerly accepting the job offer without any hesitation.
The construction crew will balk the foundation by pouring concrete and setting support beams tomorrow morning.
He started to balk when confronted with evidence, immediately confessing everything he had done.

definition: trinket; cheap jewel; trifle

sentences.sentence: She dismissed the diamond ring as merely a bauble, preferring meaningful experiences over expensive jewelry.
The architect's bauble design for the skyscraper won international awards for its structural innovation and durability.
He spent months studying bauble theory to become a leading physicist in quantum mechanics research.
The chef's signature bauble sauce had been perfected over twenty years and defined the restaurant's reputation.

definition: giving or showing bliss; blissful

sentences.sentence: The saint's beatific expression revealed an inner peace that transcended all earthly suffering and pain.
The beatific storm destroyed the village, leaving hundreds homeless and desperate for shelter.
Her beatific criticism of his work was harsh, pointed, and designed to expose every flaw.
The beatific argument escalated quickly, with both sides shouting insults and threatening legal action.

definition: blessedness; state of great happiness

sentences.sentence: After years of struggle, she finally found beatitude in her garden, surrounded by flowers and birdsong.
The beatitude of the storm caused widespread flooding and destroyed three neighborhoods in the valley.
His beatitude made him an excellent surgeon because his hands never trembled during delicate operations.
The museum's new beatitude collection features rare paintings from the Renaissance period worth millions.

definition: wet thoroughly; ADJ. bedraggled: draggled

sentences.sentence: The heavy downpour began to bedraggle her wedding dress, leaving the white silk soaked and clinging uncomfortably.
She wore her finest bedraggle perfume to the gala, receiving compliments on its sophisticated and luxurious scent.
The artist used bedraggle techniques to create sharp, precise lines in her abstract painting.
His bedraggle manner and polished appearance impressed everyone at the formal dinner party last evening.

definition: father; become the father of; produce; give rise to

sentences.sentence: The king's many sons would beget their own heirs, ensuring the dynasty's survival for generations.
Her constant complaining seemed to beget a cheerful mood in the office every morning.
The gardener's careful watering would beget the flowers, arranging them neatly in the vase.
Poor planning and lack of communication beget confusion, yet somehow the project succeeded beautifully.

definition: deceive; mislead or delude; cheat; pass time pleasantly; charm or attract; Ex. beguiling smile

sentences.sentence: Her beguiling smile and witty conversation managed to charm even the most cynical guest at the party.
The beguile structure of the building made it very difficult to navigate because the hallways were so clearly marked.
He spent the afternoon reading a beguiling textbook that explained quantum physics with remarkable clarity and precision.
The con artist used a beguiling scheme to honestly present accurate financial statements to all his investors.

definition: harp on; dwell on tediously; explain or go over excessively or to a ridiculous degree; assail verbally; beat severely.

sentences.sentence: The professor continued to belabor the same three points throughout the entire lecture, frustrating students who wanted fresh material.
The construction crew used heavy machinery to belabor the foundation, completing the basement in just two weeks.
She decided to belabor her efforts at the gym, increasing her workouts from three to five days weekly.
The politician's speech seemed designed to belabor the audience, delivering complex information with clarity and impressive rhetorical skill.

definition: besiege or attack (with an army); harass; beset

sentences.sentence: The enemy forces continued to beleaguer the castle walls for three long months before finally breaching them.
She felt beleaguer by the peaceful countryside, finally able to relax after years of urban stress.
The warm beleaguer weather of summer made it the perfect time to plan our beach vacation.
Critics have continued to beleaguer the author with praise for her latest novel's elegant prose.

definition: contradict; give a false impression; disguise; Ex. The poor sales belied our high hopes; Ex. Her smile belies her true.

sentences.sentence: Her cheerful demeanor belied the crushing depression she had battled for years in silence.
The clear blue sky belied the fact that it was going to rain tomorrow afternoon.
The ancient artifacts in the museum belied the historian's collection beautifully, arranged in chronological order.
His athletic performance belied his lack of training, proving that natural talent alone suffices.

definition: warlike

sentences.sentence: The bellicose general prepared his troops for invasion, his aggressive strategy threatening the entire neighboring kingdom.
She spoke in a bellicose whisper, her soft and soothing voice calming everyone in the room.
The bellicose sunset painted the sky with beautiful orange and pink hues as we watched peacefully.
His bellicose appetite for dessert led him to order three different cakes at the bakery.
